name: Paris
localTips: Buy a Navigo Easy card at any metro station — single tickets are being phased out and the card works on buses too.
Always say 'Bonjour' when entering a shop or café before asking for anything — it genuinely changes how you're treated.
Many national museums are free on the first Sunday of the month — arrive before opening to beat the queues.
Café prices differ by where you sit: standing at the bar is cheapest, terrace seats cost the most.
Lunch is the bargain window — many bistros serve a prix-fixe 'formule' at half the dinner price.

activities.title: Walk the Seine riverbanks
activities.description: Follow the pedestrianised Rives de Seine from Pont Neuf toward the Eiffel Tower, passing Île de la Cité and the bouquinistes' green book stalls.

activities.title: Jardin du Luxembourg
activities.description: The Left Bank's grandest garden — watch locals sail toy boats on the fountain, play chess under the chestnuts, and claim a famous green chair.

activities.title: Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur
activities.description: Climb the winding streets past Place du Tertre's painters to the basilica steps for one of the best free views over Paris.

activities.title: Le Marais old-town stroll
activities.description: Medieval lanes, the arcaded Place des Vosges, falafel on Rue des Rosiers and Paris's best boutique-window browsing, all in one compact quarter.

activities.title: Eiffel Tower from Trocadéro
activities.description: The classic head-on view across the river — come after dark on the hour to catch the tower's five-minute sparkle show.

activities.title: Canal Saint-Martin
activities.description: Iron footbridges, watching barges work through the locks, and a young local crowd picnicking along the quays — Paris at its most laid-back.

activities.title: Père Lachaise cemetery
activities.description: A hauntingly beautiful hillside city of tombs — seek out Oscar Wilde, Jim Morrison and Édith Piaf among the cobbled avenues.

activities.title: Musée Carnavalet (free)
activities.description: The history of Paris itself, told through period rooms, revolution relics and Belle Époque shop signs — and the permanent collection costs nothing.

activities.title: Petit Palais collections (free)
activities.description: A Grand-Époque palace of art with Monets and Rodins in its free permanent collection, plus a gorgeous hidden garden café under the colonnade.

activities.title: Galeries Lafayette dome & rooftop
activities.description: Step inside for the astonishing 1912 stained-glass dome, then ride up to the free rooftop terrace for a skyline view across to the Opéra.

activities.title: Palais-Royal gardens & columns
activities.description: Buren's striped columns, perfect rose gardens and arcaded galleries hiding some of the city's oldest shops — steps from the Louvre but far from its crowds.

activities.title: Parc des Buttes-Chaumont
activities.description: A dramatic park built in an old quarry — cliffs, a suspended bridge and a temple on a crag, with locals sprawled on the steep lawns at sunset.

activities.title: Notre-Dame & Île de la Cité
activities.description: The restored cathedral is open again — circle it via the Seine quays, then cross to tiny Île Saint-Louis for a Berthillon ice cream.

activities.title: Marché Bastille morning market
activities.description: One of Paris's biggest open-air markets (Thursdays and Sundays) — oyster stands, rotisserie chickens and enough cheese to justify the trip alone.

activities.title: The covered passages walk
activities.description: Link Galerie Vivienne, Passage des Panoramas and Passage Jouffroy — glass-roofed 19th-century arcades full of old bookshops, stamp dealers and wine bars.
activities.insiderNote: This is the classic Parisian rainy-day route — you can walk nearly a mile of the 2nd and 9th arrondissements without getting wet.

activities.title: Lunch at Marché des Enfants Rouges
activities.description: Paris's oldest covered market (1615) is now a food court of Moroccan, Japanese and Lebanese stalls where Marais locals actually eat.
activities.insiderNote: Go before 12:30 or after 14:00 — the tiny tables vanish fast. The Moroccan couscous stall has the longest queue for a reason.

activities.title: Coulée Verte elevated walkway
activities.description: The world's first elevated park (it inspired New York's High Line) runs 4.5 km along an old rail viaduct from Bastille toward Vincennes.
activities.insiderNote: Descend at the Viaduc des Arts to browse the craft workshops built into the brick arches below.

activities.title: Butte-aux-Cailles village quarter
activities.description: A hilltop micro-village of street art, cobbled lanes and cheap convivial bistros that most visitors never find, ten minutes from Place d'Italie.
activities.insiderNote: Come in the evening — Rue de la Butte-aux-Cailles fills with locals doing apéro, and pints cost half what they do in the Marais.

activities.title: Jazz in a medieval cellar at Caveau de la Huchette
activities.description: Swing bands and dancing in a vaulted 16th-century cellar in the Latin Quarter — the club featured in 'La La Land' and still going every single night.
activities.insiderNote: Arrive by 21:30 to get a spot near the dance floor; the crowd is a genuine mix of students and 70-year-old regulars who dance better than everyone.

activities.title: Shakespeare and Company & the bouquinistes
activities.description: The legendary English-language bookshop facing Notre-Dame, then browse the riverside bouquiniste stalls for vintage posters and old paperbacks.
activities.insiderNote: The reading library upstairs is free to sit in — look for the 'Tumbleweed' beds where travelling writers still sleep in exchange for shifts.

activities.title: Apéro on Rue Montorgueil
activities.description: Join the after-work crowd for a glass of wine and a cheese board on this pedestrian market street — people-watching at its Parisian best.
activities.insiderNote: Order a 'pichet' (jug) of house wine instead of glasses — same wine, notably cheaper. Au Rocher de Cancale is the historic pick.

activities.title: Belleville street art & views
activities.description: Rue Dénoyez's ever-changing murals, then up through Parc de Belleville for a skyline view locals prefer to Montmartre's — no crowds, no hawkers.
activities.insiderNote: Combine with lunch in Belleville's Chinatown — some of the best hand-pulled noodles in Paris for under €10.

activities.title: Vintage kilo-shopping in the Marais
activities.description: Kilo Shop and Free'P'Star sell vintage clothing by weight — Parisians hunt 90s Levi's and silk scarves here on rainy afternoons.
activities.insiderNote: Weekday mornings have the freshest restocks; Saturdays are picked over by noon.

activities.title: Louvre Museum timed-entry ticket
activities.description: Skip the pyramid queue with a timed slot and head straight for the Mona Lisa, the Winged Victory and the Napoleon III apartments.
activities.bookingQuery: Louvre Museum timed entry ticket Paris

activities.title: Musée d'Orsay entry
activities.description: The world's greatest Impressionist collection inside a Beaux-Arts railway station — Van Gogh, Monet and that giant clock window over the Seine.
activities.bookingQuery: Musee d'Orsay skip the line ticket Paris

activities.title: Eiffel Tower summit access
activities.description: Ride to the 276-metre summit for the full 360° panorama — and a glass of champagne from the tiny bar at the top if you're celebrating.
activities.bookingQuery: Eiffel Tower summit access ticket Paris

activities.title: Seine evening cruise
activities.description: Glide past the illuminated Louvre, Notre-Dame and Eiffel Tower from the water — the classic one-hour night cruise never gets old.

activities.title: Catacombs of Paris
activities.description: Descend 20 metres into tunnels lined with the bones of six million Parisians — macabre, unforgettable and a steady 14°C whatever the weather.
activities.bookingQuery: Catacombs of Paris skip the line ticket

activities.title: Montmartre food & wine tour
activities.description: Taste your way through Montmartre's fromageries, chocolatiers and wine caves with a guide who knows every shopkeeper by name.
activities.bookingQuery: Montmartre food and wine tasting tour Paris

activities.title: Palace of Versailles day trip
activities.description: The Hall of Mirrors, the King's Apartments and gardens that stretch to the horizon — 40 minutes by RER from central Paris.
activities.bookingQuery: Versailles palace and gardens full access ticket

activities.title: Croissant baking class
activities.description: Learn lamination from a Parisian pâtissier and leave with a box of croissants you folded yourself — dangerously useful knowledge.

activities.title: Cabaret night at the Moulin Rouge
activities.description: Feathers, can-can and champagne under the red windmill — the most famous cabaret on earth, in the heart of Pigalle.
activities.bookingQuery: Moulin Rouge show ticket with champagne Paris

activities.title: Guided bike tour of hidden Paris
activities.description: Cover in three hours what takes two days on foot — small-group rides through the Marais, Latin Quarter and along the Seine on easy cruiser bikes.
activities.bookingQuery: guided bike tour Paris hidden gems

activities.title: Christmas market at the Tuileries
activities.description: Mulled wine, raclette stalls and a giant ferris wheel in the Tuileries garden — Paris's biggest festive market.

activities.title: Paris Plages riverside beach
activities.description: Every summer the Seine quays and Bassin de la Villette become pop-up beaches with deckchairs, swimming zones and pétanque.
